title: Open-Weights Kimi K3 Releases to Everyone As the NSF Restricts Scientific Discovery - TCR 07/18/26
description:
Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model that beats Opus 4.8 max and GPT-5.5 high across most tasks in Simon Willison's test set and writes the low-level code that makes AI chips run faster, the FDA approved Merck's Lipfendra as the first oral cholesterol pill to match injectable efficacy, and Google signed on to anchor and finance the largest US solar-plus-storage project, a 2.5 GW center in Arkansas.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-18-2026/

0:00 Open and framing
0:48 Kimi K3 opens the frontier as a commons
5:27 Enterprises hand AI agents more autonomy than they trust
10:30 Merck's oral cholesterol pill clears the FDA
12:46 Google anchors the largest US solar project
15:28 NSF bars research collaboration with China
19:43 Close

This episode covers the Kimi K3 open-weight release and its GPU-kernel optimization, three VentureBeat surveys on enterprise AI agent autonomy and security incidents, Merck's Lipfendra oral cholesterol approval, Google's Steel River solar-plus-storage deal, and new NSF restrictions barring most NSF-funded research collaboration with China.

The day's signal is capability refusing to stay scarce at the exact moment the walls go up. Kimi K3, built by a 300-person lab, approaches 3 trillion open parameters, carries a million-token context window, and matched Fable 5 on KernelBench-Hard at making AI chips run faster - a recursive loop pointing toward cheaper, more accessible compute. On release, shares in rival labs Zhipu and MiniMax fell 27% and 16%. Merck's Lipfendra collapses the injection barrier that capped the strongest cholesterol therapy against the leading US killer, and AstraZeneca has its own oral drug in development. Google anchored and financed the largest US solar-plus-storage project to internalize its compute demand. Three enterprise surveys found half of teams shipping agents that passed every test and then failed a real customer, with scoped-identity discipline cutting incident rates from 63.5% to 40.9% - governance being hardened under load. And new NSF restrictions fence off most NSF-funded research collaboration with China, a bet that discovery can be geographically contained just as an open model proves it cannot.
